QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_32

question.text: What is the lot size for cross-currency futures (EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY) on Indian exchanges?

answer.text: 1000 units of the base currency (1000 EUR for EURUSD, 1000 GBP for GBPUSD, 1000 USD for USDJPY)
The lot size varies depending on the current exchange rate

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Cross-currency futures on Indian exchanges have a standard lot size of 1000 units of the base currency. EURUSD: 1000 EUR, GBPUSD: 1000 GBP, USDJPY: 1000 USD. Tick sizes are USD 0.0001 for EUR/GBP pairs and JPY 0.01 for USDJPY. **The Trap:** The lot size is the same 1000 units across all pairs (matching the USDINR lot of 1000 USD). It doesn't vary with exchange rates. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch3 specifies cross-currency contract specs. These are relatively less liquid than USDINR on Indian exchanges. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Cross-currency pairs on Indian exchanges allow hedging EUR, GBP, JPY exposure without INR involvement — useful for companies with purely international (non-INR) currency risk.
